Name: Space at ASU
Relevance: Overarching university initiative powering space missions, facilities, and academic alignment.

Name: ASU NewSpace
Relevance: Leads integration of academic and commercial space enterprises and houses the Planet Labs Incubator.

Name: MILO Space Science Institute
Relevance: Founded by ASU NewSpace, provides affordable and accessible space science to students and researchers.

Name: Interplanetary Initiative Lab
Relevance: Workspace for students and researchers to design, build, and test space hardware and software.

Name: Sun Devil Satellite Laboratory (SDSL)
Relevance: Designs and develops satellites, 1U CubeSats, Pulsed Plasma Thrusters, and high-altitude weather balloons.

Name: Sun Devil Rocketry (SDR)
Relevance: Student-run org dedicated to design, manufacture, and launch of high-powered solid, liquid, and hybrid rockets.

Name: Devils Ad Astra
Relevance: Interdisciplinary aerospace club developing hybrid engines and pushing for the Kármán line.

Name: Air Devils
Relevance: Fulton Student Org that designs and builds flying machines for AIAA Design/Build/Fly competitions.

Name: The Space Coalition
Relevance: Consortium of all space-focused student organizations at ASU, acting as an umbrella and resource hub.

Name: ASU Space Student Ambassador Program
Relevance: Undergraduate leadership program that networks with space sector leaders and represents ASU Space.

Name: Students for the Exploration and Development of Space (SEDS)
Relevance: Space enthusiasts participating in engineering projects and astronaut readiness training.

Name: Space Business Association
Relevance: Provides a platform for professional growth and commerce within the space exploration industry.

Name: NASA Space Grant Robotics
Relevance: Underwater robotics student organization; analog environments are highly relevant to space robotics.

Name: Buseck Center for Meteorite Studies
Relevance: Houses one of the world's largest university-based meteorite collections for planetary science research.

Name: Ronald Greeley Center for Planetary Studies
Relevance: Major NASA Regional Planetary Image Facility focusing on planetary geology.

Name: Beus Center for Cosmic Foundations
Relevance: Focuses on astrophysics and cosmology, studying the origins of the universe and cosmic structures.

Name: Beyond Center for Fundamental Concepts in Science
Relevance: Explores fundamental questions including astrobiology, the origins of life, and the universe.

Name: Cosmology Initiative
Relevance: Focuses on cosmology, the epoch of reionization, and research using the Hubble and James Webb Space Telescopes.

Name: Center for Global Discovery and Conservation Science
Relevance: Utilizes heavy remote sensing and Earth-observing satellite data for ecological and conservation mapping.

Name: Distributed Robotic Exploration and Mapping Systems Lab (DREAM)
Relevance: Focuses on multi-robot systems and mapping which has high relevance for planetary exploration.

Name: Engineering Flight Hardware Lab
Relevance: Focuses on the engineering, assembly, and testing of spaceflight hardware and instruments.

Name: Experimental Petrology and Igneous Processes Center (EPIC)
Relevance: Investigates planetary volcanism, magma oceans, and internal planetary differentiation.

Name: Hubble Data Lab
Relevance: Dedicated lab for processing and analyzing data returned by the Hubble Space Telescope.

Name: Isotope Cosmochemistry and Geochronology Lab
Relevance: Focuses on the isotopic analysis of meteorites and lunar samples to date solar system events.

Name: Laboratory for Astronomical Space Instrumentation
Relevance: Designs and develops novel instrumentation specifically for space-based astronomical observatories.

Name: Low-frequency Cosmology Lab (LoCo Lab)
Relevance: Studies the early universe and the first stars using low-frequency radio astronomy techniques.

Name: Lunar Polar Hydrogen Mapper Development and Test Lab
Relevance: Developed and tested the LunaH-Map CubeSat mission to map water ice on the Moon.

Name: Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter Camera Science Operations Center
Relevance: Operates the LROC instrument on NASA's Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter and processes lunar imagery.

Name: Mars Space Flight Facility
Relevance: Operates instruments on Mars missions (like THEMIS, TES) and manages Mars data exploration.

Name: Mission Operations Facility
Relevance: Provides command, control, and data downlinks for ASU-led space missions and instruments.

Name: OSIRIS-REx Thermal Emission Spectrometer Lab
Relevance: Dedicated to the operation and data analysis of the OTES instrument on the OSIRIS-REx asteroid sample return mission.

Name: Planetary Aeolian Lab
Relevance: Houses wind tunnels used to simulate the atmospheric conditions of Mars, Venus, and Titan.

Name: Planetary GIS Lab
Relevance: Develops geographic information systems and maps for planetary surfaces.

Name: Planetary Surface Imaging Science Lab
Relevance: Focuses on multispectral imaging and analysis of planetary surfaces across the solar system.

Name: Planetary Surfaces Spectroscopy Lab
Relevance: Utilizes spectroscopy to determine the mineralogical composition of other planets.

Name: Terahertz Instrumentation Lab
Relevance: Develops high-frequency sensors and instrumentation relevant to astrophysics and planetary science.

Name: Amateur Radio Club (W7ASU)
Relevance: Facilitates amateur radio which is crucial for CubeSat and high-altitude balloon tracking and telemetry.
